summary: Dependency-confusion exfiltration: preinstall posts host/env data to attacker AWS API Gateway
details: @doaction/shared@99.99.99 declares a preinstall hook (`"preinstall": "node bin/postinstall.js"` in package.json) that runs unconditionally on `npm install`. The script harvests host and user identifiers (os.hostname(), HOSTNAME, COMPUTERNAME, USER, USERNAME, HOME, PWD, PATH, SHELL, AWS_REGION, Kubernetes service host/port, and other environment variables) and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded endpoint at https://e1gjv2ne5a.execute-api.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/v1/input (src/index.js ~line 155). The README frames this as 'safe environment telemetry with Datadog integration', but the destination is an anonymous AWS API Gateway, not Datadog — a deceptive cover story. The package coordinate (scoped `@doaction/shared` published to public npm at version 99.99.99 with `publishConfig.access: restricted`) is the canonical dependency-confusion shape, designed to outrank an organization's internal private package of the same name. Any developer or build agent that resolves this version from public npm leaks host and environment metadata to the attacker on install. The collected fields include credentials-adjacent context (AWS_REGION, K8s in-cluster endpoints, PATH, SHELL) useful for follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
